data.content: I can see the project — the express test expects `15.00` but the code returns `10.00`. Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test with hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. This is a .NET 8 MSTest project. I'll start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can see the test results and iterate.

Let me first run the tests to see the current state, then set up dotnet watch.

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running. Here's the current state:

- ✅ **Passed:** `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5`
- ❌ **Failed:** `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — expects `15.00` but `CalculateShipping("express", ...)` returns `10.00`

The watcher is live and waiting. When you edit `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`, it will automatically rebuild and re-run tests. To fix the failing test, change line 23 from `10.00m` to `15.00m` for the `"express"` case.
